sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.93 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: A group$G$is called a if for any finite subgroup$H G$any two conjugate elements of prime order in$N_G(H)/H$generate a finite subgroup. Are the following well-known results of the theory of finite groups true in the class of (periodic) Shunkov groups? [r]{a)} The Baer--Suzuki theorem (see 11.11 in Archive). [r]{b)} The Burnside--Brauer--Suzuki theorem on the existence of a normal section of order~2...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
